raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a causal law linking moral quality of thoughts to physical bodily outcomes",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "thoughts possess direct causal power over physiological states",
    "moral valence of thoughts ('unlawful' vs. 'glad and beautiful') determines health or decay",
    "the effect occurs rapidly and visibly",
    "the body is subordinate to and shaped by non-physical mental commands"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent within an idealist framework where mind is ontologically prior to matter; the binary opposition of thought types maps cleanly onto binary bodily outcomes",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "belief in the claim produces measurable psychological or behavioral changes that improve well-being, or the statement functions as an effective ethical heuristic encouraging mental discipline",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the claim is interpreted as a literal, exceptionless physical law, leading individuals to attribute disease solely to moral or mental failings while disregarding genetic, environmental, or infectious causes"
}
